name: alex-cognitive-architecture
description: Strap a rocket to your back. Take Your CODE to New Heights with an AI that remembers & grows.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Core commands available; architecture initialization requires workspace trust

commands.title: Alex: Convert to Word with Options...
Alex: Convert Markdown to Gamma Slides
Alex: Inherit Skill from Global Knowledge
Alex: Propose Skill to Global Knowledge

command.description: The Alex cognitive command to execute (e.g. alex.meditate, alex.dream)

alex.workspace.protectedMode.description: Enable protected mode to prevent Alex from modifying .github/ in this workspace. Enable this for Master Alex development environment.

alex.workspace.autoProtectMasterAlex.description: Automatically enable protected mode when Alex extension source code (platforms/vscode-extension/) is detected in workspace.

alex.globalKnowledge.repoPath.description: Custom path to AI-Memory folder. Leave empty for auto-detection (OneDrive/AI-Memory or ~/.alex/AI-Memory).

enumDescriptions: Auto — let persona detection choose communication style
alex.personalityMode.description: Control Alex's communication style. Auto adapts to the detected project persona.

languageModelTools.name: alex_cognitive_synapse_health
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Scans Alex cognitive architecture memory files and validates synaptic connections. Reports broken connections and provides health metrics. Use when checking architecture health or before/after meditation.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Check the health of Alex's synaptic connections

detailed.description: Include detailed list of broken connections

languageModelTools.name: alex_cognitive_memory_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Searches Alex's memory files (procedural, episodic, domain knowledge) for relevant information. UNCONSCIOUS BEHAVIOR: Automatically searches global knowledge base when local search finds nothing, retrieving cross-project learnings transparently.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search Alex's memory files for information

query.description: The search query to find in memory files

languageModelTools.name: alex_cognitive_architecture_status
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the current status of Alex cognitive architecture including version, installed memory files, and available commands. Use to check if Alex is properly configured.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get Alex architecture status and configuration

includeMetrics.description: Include detailed metrics about memory files

languageModelTools.name: alex_platform_mcp_recommendations
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Provides recommendations for Azure and M365 MCP tools based on the development scenario. Use when the user needs guidance on which MCP tools to use for Azure or Microsoft 365 development.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Get MCP tool recommendations for Azure/M365 development

scenario.description: The development scenario to get recommendations for

languageModelTools.name: alex_cognitive_user_profile
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Manages the user's personal profile and preferences for personalized interactions. Can get, update, or check if a profile exists. Use to learn about the user and customize Alex's communication style.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Manage your personal preferences for Alex interactions

action.description: Action to perform: get profile, update a field, or check if profile exists

field.description: The profile field to update (for update action)

value.description: The new value for the field (for update action)

languageModelTools.name: alex_cognitive_self_actualization
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Runs comprehensive self-assessment of Alex cognitive architecture: validates synaptic connections, checks version consistency, assesses memory architecture balance, generates improvement recommendations, and creates meditation session documentation. Use when user asks to 'self-actualize', 'deep meditate', or wants comprehensive architecture assessment.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Run comprehensive self-assessment and deep meditation

createReport.description: Create a meditation session record file

autoFix.description: Automatically fix minor issues if found

languageModelTools.name: alex_knowledge_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Searches Alex's global knowledge base for patterns and insights learned across ALL projects. Use when looking for cross-project learnings, reusable patterns, or insights that might apply to the current problem. Returns relevant knowledge from any project the user has worked on.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search knowledge learned from all your projects

query.description: The search query to find in global knowledge

type.description: Type of knowledge to search: pattern (reusable), insight (specific learning), or all

languageModelTools.name: alex_knowledge_save_insight
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Saves a new insight or learning to Alex's global knowledge base. Use when the user has learned something valuable that could help in other projects.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Save a learning to your global knowledge base

title.description: A clear, descriptive title for this insight

insight.description: The main insight or learning content

problem.description: The problem context that led to this insight

languageModelTools.name: alex_knowledge_promote
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Promotes a project-local domain knowledge file (DK-*.md) to the global knowledge base.

filePath.description: Full path to the DK-*.md file to promote

languageModelTools.name: alex_knowledge_status
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Shows the current status of Alex's global knowledge base: total patterns, insights, categories, recent entries, known projects, and known categories.

languageModelTools.name: alex_quality_heir_validation
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Scans heir content (.github/ in vscode-extension package) for personal data leaks and quality issues. Returns content for semantic analysis by the LLM. Use before publishing or after heir sync to validate content is safe for Marketplace distribution.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Validate heir content is safe for distribution

scope.description: Validate specific folder or all heir content

includeContent.description: Return full content for LLM semantic analysis

languageModelTools.name: alex_cognitive_state_update
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Updates Alex's visual cognitive state in the welcome sidebar. Call this when you detect a shift in the user's activity context (e.g., debugging, building, planning, reviewing, learning, teaching, meditation, dream, discovery) OR when activating an agent mode (builder, researcher, validator, documentarian, azure, m365). This changes Alex's avatar to match the current cognitive mode or agent. Lightweight — no confirmation needed.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Update Alex's cognitive state and avatar

state.description: The cognitive state or agent mode to switch to. Cognitive states: debugging (fixing errors), planning (architecture/design), building (implementing features), reviewing (code review/audit), learning (understanding/exploring), teaching (explaining/mentoring), meditation (reflection/consolidation), dream (neural maintenance), discovery (breakthroughs/insights). Agent modes: builder, researcher, validator, documentarian, azure, m365. Special: persona (reset to project-appropriate avatar after protocols).

languageModelTools.name: alex_cognitive_cross_domain_synthesis
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Analyzes episodic memories across different knowledge domains to surface unexpected connections. Reads meditation and dream reports from .github/episodic/, classifies each by domain, maps the existing synapse network, and identifies domain pairs with few or zero connections. Returns synthesis candidates with opportunity scores. Use during meditation Phase 1.7 to evolve from consolidation to generation of new cross-domain insights.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Find unexpected connections between knowledge domains

focusDomain.description: Optional: focus synthesis on connections involving this specific domain (e.g., 'data', 'cognitive', 'vscode', 'azure')

walkthroughs.title: Getting Started with Alex
walkthroughs.description: Set up your cognitive learning partner and start building knowledge

steps.title: Meet Alex
steps.description:
Alex is your cognitive learning partner — an AI architecture that learns, remembers, and grows with you across projects.

Alex has **160 skills**, **persistent memory**, and **cross-project knowledge sharing**.

steps.title: Initialize Architecture
steps.description:
Deploy the cognitive architecture to your workspace. This creates memory files, skills, and synaptic connections.

[Initialize Architecture](command:alex.initialize)

steps.title: Chat with Alex
steps.description:
Open GitHub Copilot Chat in Agent Mode. Alex's skills, instructions, and tools activate automatically.

Try asking: 'Check my architecture health' or 'Run a meditation session'.

steps.title: Set Up Your Profile
steps.description:
Personalize Alex by setting your learning preferences, expertise areas, and communication style.

Try asking: 'Show my profile' in agent mode.

steps.title: Consolidate Knowledge
steps.description:
Consolidate what you've learned into persistent memory files.

Try asking: 'Meditate on what I learned about design patterns today' in agent mode.

steps.title: Run Neural Maintenance
steps.description:
Keep Alex healthy by running the Dream protocol — it validates synapses and repairs broken connections.

[Run Dream Protocol](command:alex.dream)

walkthroughs.title: Global Knowledge Setup
walkthroughs.description: Share learnings across all your projects with cloud-synced AI-Memory

steps.title: What is Global Knowledge?
steps.description:
Global Knowledge lets Alex share patterns and insights across all your projects. Learnings from one project automatically benefit every other project.

It's powered by AI-Memory — a cloud-synced folder (OneDrive/iCloud/Dropbox) that travels with you across workspaces.

steps.title: AI-Memory Location
steps.description:
Alex auto-detects your AI-Memory folder in common cloud storage locations.

The folder contains your profile, global knowledge patterns, insights, and learning goals.

steps.title: First Insight
steps.description:
Once set up, try saving a global insight after discovering something useful.

Insights are stored in AI-Memory and available across all your workspaces.

steps.title: Explore Knowledge
steps.description:
View your accumulated knowledge and patterns.

Try asking: 'Search my global knowledge' in agent mode.

scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run lint
scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run sync-architecture && npm run clean && npm run quality-gate && npm run package
